join_points.validation_target: Independent Windows, macOS, and Linux core/stdio/bundle evidence cells for one immutable build identity
join_points.pass_condition: Every required OS cell has an honest terminal status and no OS result is inferred from another OS.
join_points.recovery: Keep failed/blocked/unsupported cells exact, route product defects to their owning task, and do not start host claim work from synthetic or stale cells.

join_points.validation_target: Separate real Codex and Claude install/routing/lifecycle/takeover evidence
join_points.pass_condition: Each host/version/OS/scope cell stands on its own real-host receipt; neither host substitutes for the other.
join_points.recovery: Withhold only the unsupported host cells, preserve exact failure receipts, and reopen architecture only if the approved bundled-runtime or shared-state model is contradicted.

validation.name: Registry, schema, result, and exclusion contract suite
assertions: closed recursive schemas and forbidden-input rejection
all operation definitions have handler/schema/policy/contract refs
exact nine-field result with outcome/resource/effects invariants
deferred/dropped/CLI-only IDs absent from public projections
validation.evidence: Machine-readable schema/catalog validation and per-operation case matrix.

validation.name: Real CLI compatibility and CLI/MCP semantic equivalence
assertions: existing CLI schemaVersion 1 fixtures/compact/text/exit codes remain
allowed surface/runtime path/generated ID differences only
validation.evidence: Raw CLI JSON/exit code, real MCP structured result, normalized diff, and state/effect receipts.

validation.name: Project scope, dynamic risk, ownership, and compatibility refusal matrix
validation.evidence: Typed results, no-effect snapshots, external fixture survival, and adapter equivalence receipts.

validation.name: Task lifecycle reuse, concurrency, and ownership suite
validation.evidence: Append-only spawn/signal receipts, run/state records, port observations, and normalized results.

validation.name: Journal crash, retention, correlation, and no-replay suite
assertions: fault before prepared has no effect
validation.evidence: Durable records/revisions/index receipts, independent handler/effect counters, and public recovery transcripts.

validation.name: Safe-clean digest and protected-state suite
validation.evidence: Before/after tree digests, plan payload/digest, journal transitions, and refusal/positive results.

validation.name: adoption.inspect bounded zero-write suite
validation.evidence: CLI/MCP outputs plus before/after state/process/port receipts and exclusion scan.

validation.name: Bounded observation cursor/redaction suite
assertions: no arbitrary paths or permanent follow
malformed record warning without mutation authority
validation.evidence: Cursor fixtures, page transcripts, redaction assertions, and no-effect receipts.

validation.name: Real MCP stdio protocol suite
validation.evidence: SDK-client transcript, byte-level stdout/stderr capture, runtime/build identity, and journal/state receipts.

validation.name: Canonical Skill trace and forbidden-call evaluation
validation.kind: deterministic eval plus real host trace
assertions: pre-dispatch unavailable/unsupported safe CLI fallback only
validation.evidence: Machine-readable tool/command traces and host discovery/routing receipts.

validation.name: Skill installation and migration matrix
assertions: current and legacy Codex plus project/Plugin copies
validation.evidence: Before/after trees, content-manifest digests, installer/doctor results, and user-content preservation receipts.

validation.name: Compatibility, build identity, and tamper suite
validation.evidence: Golden canonical payloads, manifest validation, tamper matrix, capabilities and fail-closed results.

validation.name: Deterministic Plugin generation and isolated bundle suite
validation.kind: automated build plus native OS execution
validation.evidence: Build/metafile/archive/integrity receipts and BUNDLE-* cells for each OS.

validation.name: Host-agnostic Plugin lifetime and compatibility chain
validation.kind: automated state/process contract plus host precondition
assertions: compatible build B same run identity
validation.evidence: One chained run/state/ownership/build timeline; does not replace VT-015 or VT-016 host evidence.

validation.name: Real Codex install, routing, lifecycle, and takeover
validation.evidence: CODEX-INSTALL-* and CODEX-LIFECYCLE-* cells; status starts not_executed_host_owned.

validation.name: Real Claude Code install, routing, lifecycle, reload, and takeover
validation.evidence: CLAUDE-INSTALL-* and CLAUDE-LIFECYCLE-* cells; status starts not_executed_host_owned.

validation.name: Exact Windows/macOS/Linux evidence matrix
validation.claim_gate: Every placeholder is replaced with recorded exact values at execution; every cell begins not executed and proves only itself.

validation.name: Evidence registry invalidation and readiness-rollup refusal
assertions: capabilities/docs expose exact evidence refs only
validation.evidence: Rollup fixtures with intentional missing/stale cells and no broad ready boolean.

validation.name: Deferred/dropped capability exclusion guards
assertions: adoption.apply and medium-risk Agent execution absent

validation.name: Repository baseline and published-entrypoint regression
assertions: existing smoke/package checks exposed by live package scripts
validation.evidence: Command receipts, package inventory, CLI fixture diff, and repository-root provenance.

required_refs.purpose: Representative real-entrypoint journeys and evidence matrix

transition.recovery: Return to /sp.tasks for graph-only repair; return to /sp.plan or /sp.clarify only when an accepted architecture or scope contract is contradicted.
